title: DisjointHardInterventions
tex_block_html: Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $W_1 \ins J \cup V$ and $W_2 \ins V$ two disjoint subsets of nodes of $G$. Then the CDMG obtained from first hard intervening on $W_1$ and then node-splitting $W_2$ is the same CDMG that arises from first node-splitting $W_2$ and then hard intervening on $W_1$. \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)} = \lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}. \]

\def\rowref{claim\_3\_8}
\phantomsection\label{claim_3_8}

\begin{Lem}[Disjoint hard interventions and node-splittings commute]\label{disjoint-hard-interventions-node-splittings-commute}
    Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def \ref{def-cdmg}); throughout we use the notation of def \ref{not-cdmg} and recall the typing and axioms of def \ref{def-cdmg}, namely $J \cap V = \emptyset$, $E \subseteq (J \cup V) \times V$, $L \subseteq V \times V$, $L$ irreflexive ($(v_1, v_2) \in L \Rightarrow v_1 \neq v_2$), and $L$ symmetric ($(v_1, v_2) \in L \Leftrightarrow (v_2, v_1) \in L$). Let
    \[ W_1 \subseteq J \cup V \quad\text{and}\quad W_2 \subseteq V \]
    be two subsets of nodes of $G$, subject to the disjointness side condition
    \[ W_1 \cap W_2 \;=\; \emptyset. \]
    All three quantifiers ``for every CDMG $G$'', ``for every $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$'', and ``for every $W_2 \subseteq V$ with $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$'' are read \emph{universally}; in particular the (vacuously disjoint) case $W_1 = W_2 = \emptyset$ is admitted.

    \emph{Well-typedness of the inner splitting on the LHS.}
    The LHS $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}$ requires its outer $\spl(W_2)$ to act on a CDMG whose output-node set contains $W_2$, i.e.\ $W_2 \subseteq V_{\doit(W_1)}$ per def \ref{def:G_node-splitting}'s precondition. By item ii.\ of def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} we have $V_{\doit(W_1)} = V \sm W_1$, and the inclusion
    \[ W_2 \;\subseteq\; V \sm W_1 \;=\; V_{\doit(W_1)} \]
    follows from $W_2 \subseteq V$ together with $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$. Hence the LHS is defined per def \ref{def:G_node-splitting}. Well-typedness of the RHS $\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}$ is spelled out in the carrier-reading paragraph below the displayed equality.

    \emph{The conclusion.}
    The CDMG obtained from first hard intervening on $W_1$ and then node-splitting $W_2$ equals the CDMG obtained from first node-splitting $W_2$ and then hard intervening on $W_1$:
    \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)} \;=\; \lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}. \]
    The equality is understood componentwise on the four components $(J, V, E, L)$ of def \ref{def-cdmg}; the conjunctive unpacking into the four field-by-field equalities is deferred to the proof.

    \medskip\emph{Carrier reading of the equality.}
    By def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} the operation $\spl(W_2)$ shifts the carrier from $J \cup V$ to the tagged-disjoint-union carrier
    \[ J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)} \;=\; J \;\cup\; \lp (V \sm W_2) \dcup W_2^0 \dcup W_2^1 \rp, \]
    while by def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} the operation $\doit(W)$ preserves the carrier $J \cup V$ of its input as a set (items i.\ and ii.\ give $J_{\doit(W)} \cup V_{\doit(W)} = (J \cup W) \cup (V \sm W) = J \cup V$ whenever $W \subseteq J \cup V$). Thus both sides of the displayed equality live in a CDMG over the same $\spl(W_2)$-carrier just displayed, and the asserted equality is a \emph{literal} $=$ of CDMGs (def \ref{def-cdmg}) — \emph{not} an equality up to a carrier-relabelling map. On the right-hand side, $\doit(W_1)$ is read as hard-intervening on the \emph{unsplit copies} of the nodes of $W_1$ inside the split-graph carrier: every $w \in W_1$ satisfies $w \in J \cup V$ (by $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$) and $w \notin W_2$ (by $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$), so $w \in J \cup (V \sm W_2)$, which is the untagged piece of $J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)}$ under def \ref{def:G_node-splitting}'s notational shorthand ``$v^0 := v^1 := v$ for $v \in J \cup (V \sm W_2)$'' (i.e.\ $W_1$ injects into the split-graph carrier as the unsplit injection $w \mapsto w$). The precondition $W_1 \subseteq J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)}$ of def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} is thereby met by the unsplit copies, so the RHS is also defined per def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention}, and the LN's $G_{\spl(W_2)}$ carrier reading agrees with the unique LN-faithful reading of $\doit(W_1)$ on it.
\end{Lem}

\end{document}

\begin{Lem}[Disjoint hard interventions and node-splittings commute]\label{disjoint-hard-interventions-node-splittings-commute}
    Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def \ref{def-cdmg}); throughout we use the notation of def \ref{not-cdmg} and recall the typing and axioms of def \ref{def-cdmg}, namely $J \cap V = \emptyset$, $E \subseteq (J \cup V) \times V$, and $L$ consists of unordered pairs of distinct vertices in $V$. Let
    \[ W_1 \subseteq J \cup V \quad\text{and}\quad W_2 \subseteq V \]
    be two subsets of nodes of $G$, subject to the disjointness side condition
    \[ W_1 \cap W_2 \;=\; \emptyset. \]
    All three quantifiers ``for every CDMG $G$'', ``for every $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$'', and ``for every $W_2 \subseteq V$ with $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$'' are read \emph{universally}; in particular the (vacuously disjoint) case $W_1 = W_2 = \emptyset$ is admitted.

    \emph{Well-typedness of the inner splitting on the LHS.}
    The LHS $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}$ requires its outer $\spl(W_2)$ to act on a CDMG whose output-node set contains $W_2$, i.e.\ $W_2 \subseteq V_{\doit(W_1)}$ per def \ref{def:G_node-splitting}'s precondition. By item ii.\ of def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} we have $V_{\doit(W_1)} = V \sm W_1$, and the inclusion
    \[ W_2 \;\subseteq\; V \sm W_1 \;=\; V_{\doit(W_1)} \]
    follows from $W_2 \subseteq V$ together with $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$. Hence the LHS is defined per def \ref{def:G_node-splitting}. Well-typedness of the RHS $\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}$ is spelled out in the carrier-reading paragraph below the displayed equality.

    \emph{The conclusion.}
    The CDMG obtained from first hard intervening on $W_1$ and then node-splitting $W_2$ equals the CDMG obtained from first node-splitting $W_2$ and then hard intervening on $W_1$:
    \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)} \;=\; \lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}. \]
    The equality is understood componentwise on the four components $(J, V, E, L)$ of def \ref{def-cdmg}; the conjunctive unpacking into the four field-by-field equalities is deferred to the proof.

    \medskip\emph{Carrier reading of the equality.}
    By def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} the operation $\spl(W_2)$ shifts the carrier from $J \cup V$ to the tagged-disjoint-union carrier
    \[ J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)} \;=\; J \;\cup\; \lp (V \sm W_2) \dcup W_2^0 \dcup W_2^1 \rp, \]
    while by def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} the operation $\doit(W)$ preserves the carrier $J \cup V$ of its input as a set (items i.\ and ii.\ give $J_{\doit(W)} \cup V_{\doit(W)} = (J \cup W) \cup (V \sm W) = J \cup V$ whenever $W \subseteq J \cup V$). Thus both sides of the displayed equality live in a CDMG over the same $\spl(W_2)$-carrier just displayed, and the asserted equality is a \emph{literal} $=$ of CDMGs (def \ref{def-cdmg}) --- \emph{not} an equality up to a carrier-relabelling map. On the right-hand side, $\doit(W_1)$ is read as hard-intervening on the \emph{unsplit copies} of the nodes of $W_1$ inside the split-graph carrier: every $w \in W_1$ satisfies $w \in J \cup V$ (by $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$) and $w \notin W_2$ (by $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$), so $w \in J \cup (V \sm W_2)$, which is the untagged piece of $J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)}$ under def \ref{def:G_node-splitting}'s notational shorthand ``$v^0 := v^1 := v$ for $v \in J \cup (V \sm W_2)$'' (i.e.\ $W_1$ injects into the split-graph carrier as the unsplit injection $w \mapsto w$). The precondition $W_1 \subseteq J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)}$ of def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} is thereby met by the unsplit copies, so the RHS is also defined per def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention}, and the LN's $G_{\spl(W_2)}$ carrier reading agrees with the unique LN-faithful reading of $\doit(W_1)$ on it.
\end{Lem}

% --- proof ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Proof body adapted from the LN's \Claude{...} block at
% lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex lines 504-534, with the following
% refinements (carried over verbatim from the pre-refactor twin, except
% for the bidirected-edge refactor-context note flagged in (iii)):
% (i) the LN's terse parenthetical "$W_1 \cap W_2^0 = W_1 \cap W_2^1 =
%     \emptyset$" is replaced with an explicit decomposition of
%     $((V \sm W_2) \dcup W_2^0 \dcup W_2^1) \sm W_1$ that splits the
%     intersection across the three disjoint pieces and cites the
%     disjointness $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$ at the $(V \sm W_2)$-piece;
% (ii) the LN's "head $v_2^0 \in W_1$ iff $v_2 \in W_1$" claim is unpacked
%      into two sub-cases on whether $v_2 \in W_2$ or $v_2 \in V \sm W_2$,
%      with the disjointness $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$ cross-checked in
%      the $v_2 \in W_2$ branch;
% (iii) in the post-refactor `Sym2`-typed encoding the LN's literal
%       one-sided removal clause translates directly to "every endpoint
%       of the unordered pair lies outside $W_1$" (there is no ordered
%       "second component" to filter on), so the deviation paragraph
%       carried by the pre-refactor twin is no longer needed; it is
%       replaced here by a short refactor-context note, and the
%       calculation that follows uses endpoint-universal wording for the
%       single removal condition "$v_1, v_2 \notin W_1$".
% The proof is organised under the LN's three section headings
% (Node sets / Directed edges / Bidirected edges); the "Node sets" section
% covers the $J$ and $V$ fields jointly, matching the LN's grouping and
% the four-field componentwise check of the downstream Lean proof.
\begin{proof}
We verify the equality $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)} = \lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}$ componentwise on the four fields $(J, V, E, L)$ of def \ref{def-cdmg}, organised as in the LN under three section headings: ``Node sets'' (covering $J$ and $V$ jointly), ``Directed edges'' (the $E$ field), and ``Bidirected edges'' (the $L$ field). The well-typedness of both iteration orders has been established in the statement block above: the LHS by $W_2 \subseteq V \sm W_1 = V_{\doit(W_1)}$ (using $W_2 \subseteq V$ and the disjointness $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$), and the RHS by $W_1 \subseteq J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)}$ via the unsplit injection of $W_1$ into the split-graph carrier (using $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$ and $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$).

\smallskip\noindent\emph{Notational reminder.}
For $w \in W_2$ we write $w^0$, $w^1$ for the two tagged copies produced by $\spl(W_2)$ per def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} item ii.; these live in the tagged-disjoint-union pieces $W_2^0$ and $W_2^1$ respectively, and are formally distinct from every element of $J \cup V$ (and from each other), per def \ref{def:G_node-splitting}'s tagged-copies disjointness assertions $W_2^0 \cap V = W_2^1 \cap V = W_2^0 \cap J = W_2^1 \cap J = W_2^0 \cap W_2^1 = \emptyset$. For $v \in J \cup (V \sm W_2)$ we use def \ref{def:G_node-splitting}'s unsplit shorthand $v^0 := v^1 := v$, which leaves $v$ in the untagged piece of $J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)}$. On the right-hand side, the outer $\doit(W_1)$ is read on the lifted intervention set ``$W_1$ inside the split-graph carrier'', which by the carrier-reading paragraph of the statement is the unsplit image $\lC w \st w \in W_1 \rC \subseteq J \cup (V \sm W_2)$ (using $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$ and $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$); throughout the proof we write $W_1$ for this lifted set, using the unsplit shorthand $w = w^0 = w^1$ for $w \in W_1$. ``Head'' of a directed edge $(a, b) \in E$ means its second component $b$; ``endpoints'' of a bidirected edge $v_1 \huh v_2 \in L$ means the unordered pair $\{v_1, v_2\}$ of its two vertices.

\medskip\noindent\emph{Node sets.}

\smallskip\noindent\emph{Input nodes ($J$).}
By def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} item i.\ applied to $G$ with intervention set $W_1$,
\[ J_{G_{\doit(W_1)}} \;=\; J \cup W_1. \]
By def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} item i.\ applied to $G_{\doit(W_1)}$ with split set $W_2$ (well-typed by $W_2 \subseteq V \sm W_1 = V_{\doit(W_1)}$),
\[ J_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}} \;=\; J_{G_{\doit(W_1)}} \;=\; J \cup W_1. \]
For the RHS, by def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} item i.\ applied to $G$ with split set $W_2$ and then def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} item i.\ applied to $G_{\spl(W_2)}$ with intervention set $W_1$ (lifted via the unsplit injection),
\[ J_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}} \;=\; J_{G_{\spl(W_2)}} \cup W_1 \;=\; J \cup W_1. \]
Hence
\[ J_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}} \;=\; J \cup W_1 \;=\; J_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}}. \]

\smallskip\noindent\emph{Output nodes ($V$).}
By def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} item ii.\ applied to $G$ with intervention set $W_1$ and then def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} item ii.\ applied to $G_{\doit(W_1)}$ with split set $W_2 \subseteq V \sm W_1 = V_{\doit(W_1)}$,
\[
    V_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}}
    \;=\; \lp V_{G_{\doit(W_1)}} \sm W_2 \rp \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1
    \;=\; \lp (V \sm W_1) \sm W_2 \rp \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1,
\]
which simplifies (by $(V \sm W_1) \sm W_2 = V \sm W_1 \sm W_2$, the standard ``two-step removal'' identity for set-difference) to
\[
    V_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}}
    \;=\; (V \sm W_1 \sm W_2) \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1.
\]

For the RHS, by def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} item ii.\ applied to $G$ with split set $W_2$ and then def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} item ii.\ applied to $G_{\spl(W_2)}$ with intervention set $W_1$ (lifted to the split-graph carrier via the unsplit injection),
\[
    V_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}}
    \;=\; V_{G_{\spl(W_2)}} \sm W_1
    \;=\; \lp (V \sm W_2) \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1 \rp \sm W_1.
\]
We simplify the latter by decomposing the set-difference across the three disjoint pieces of the disjoint union, using both the disjointness hypothesis $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$ (at the untagged piece) and def \ref{def:G_node-splitting}'s tagged-construction disjointness (at the tagged pieces):
\begin{itemize}
    \item $W_1 \cap W_2^0 = \emptyset$ and $W_1 \cap W_2^1 = \emptyset$: by def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} item ii.'s tagged-construction disjointness, $W_2^0$ and $W_2^1$ satisfy $W_2^0 \cap (J \cup V) = W_2^1 \cap (J \cup V) = \emptyset$. Since $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$ (and the lifted $W_1$ in the split-graph carrier sits in the untagged piece $J \cup (V \sm W_2)$, which is a subset of $J \cup V$ under the unsplit injection), the lifted $W_1$ does not meet $W_2^0$ or $W_2^1$. Hence the set-difference acts as the identity on both tagged pieces.
    \item $(V \sm W_2) \sm W_1 = V \sm W_1 \sm W_2$: indeed, for any $x$, the chain of equivalences $x \in (V \sm W_2) \sm W_1$ iff $x \in V$, $x \notin W_2$, $x \notin W_1$ iff $x \in V$, $x \notin W_1$, $x \notin W_2$ iff $x \in V \sm W_1 \sm W_2$ holds. (Here the disjointness $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$ is \emph{not} needed for this equality; the two iterated removals commute by associativity of $\sm$ alone.)
\end{itemize}
Combining the three piecewise calculations,
\[
    \lp (V \sm W_2) \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1 \rp \sm W_1
    \;=\; \lp (V \sm W_2) \sm W_1 \rp \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1
    \;=\; (V \sm W_1 \sm W_2) \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1.
\]
Hence
\[
    V_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}}
    \;=\; (V \sm W_1 \sm W_2) \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1
    \;=\; V_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}}.
\]
\checkmark

\medskip\noindent\emph{Directed edges.}
By def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} item iii.\ applied to $G$ with intervention set $W_1$,
\[
    E_{G_{\doit(W_1)}}
    \;=\; E \sm \lC (v_1, v_2) \in E \st v_2 \in W_1 \rC
    \;=\; \lC (v_1, v_2) \in E \st v_2 \notin W_1 \rC.
\]
By def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} item iii.\ applied to $G_{\doit(W_1)}$ with split set $W_2$ (each surviving directed edge $(v_1, v_2) \in E_{G_{\doit(W_1)}}$ is lifted to $(v_1^1, v_2^0)$, and each $w \in W_2$ contributes a transfer edge $(w^0, w^1)$),
\begin{align*}
    E_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}}
    &= \lC (v_1^1, v_2^0) \st (v_1, v_2) \in E_{G_{\doit(W_1)}} \rC \;\cup\; \lC (w^0, w^1) \st w \in W_2 \rC \\
    &= \lC (v_1^1, v_2^0) \st (v_1, v_2) \in E,\; v_2 \notin W_1 \rC \;\cup\; \lC (w^0, w^1) \st w \in W_2 \rC.
\end{align*}

For the RHS, by def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} item iii.\ applied to $G$ with split set $W_2$,
\[
    E_{G_{\spl(W_2)}}
    \;=\; \lC (v_1^1, v_2^0) \st (v_1, v_2) \in E \rC \;\cup\; \lC (w^0, w^1) \st w \in W_2 \rC.
\]
By def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} item iii.\ applied to $G_{\spl(W_2)}$ with intervention set $W_1$ (lifted to the split-graph carrier via the unsplit injection),
\[
    E_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}}
    \;=\; E_{G_{\spl(W_2)}} \sm \lC (a, b) \in E_{G_{\spl(W_2)}} \st b \in W_1 \rC.
\]
The removal condition ``$b \in W_1$'' inspects the head $b$ of each candidate generator. We analyse it case by case on the two generator types of $E_{G_{\spl(W_2)}}$.

\begin{itemize}
    \item \emph{Lifted edges $(v_1^1, v_2^0)$ from $(v_1, v_2) \in E$.}
    The head is $v_2^0$. By def \ref{def-cdmg}'s typing $E \subseteq (J \cup V) \times V$ we have $v_2 \in V$, so we case-split on whether $v_2 \in W_2$:
    \begin{itemize}
        \item \emph{If $v_2 \in W_2$:} $v_2^0 \in W_2^0$ is a tagged copy, lying in the disjoint-union piece $W_2^0$. By def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} item ii.'s tagged-construction disjointness $W_2^0 \cap (J \cup V) = \emptyset$, and since $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$, we have $v_2^0 \notin W_1$ (read in the split-graph carrier via the unsplit lift). The edge \emph{survives} the RHS removal. Cross-check with the LHS: by the disjointness hypothesis $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$, $v_2 \in W_2$ also forces $v_2 \notin W_1$, so the LHS-side condition ``$v_2 \notin W_1$'' is automatically met --- both sides keep this edge.
        \item \emph{If $v_2 \in V \sm W_2$:} $v_2^0 = v_2$ (unsplit shorthand, since $v_2 \in V \sm W_2 \subseteq J \cup (V \sm W_2)$). The lifted $W_1$ in the split-graph carrier is the unsplit image $\lC w \st w \in W_1 \rC$, so $v_2^0 \in W_1$ iff $v_2 \in W_1$ (the unsplit injection $J \cup (V \sm W_2) \hookrightarrow J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)}$ is injective). The edge is removed by the RHS iff $v_2 \in W_1$, equivalently kept iff $v_2 \notin W_1$. This matches the LHS condition ``$v_2 \notin W_1$'' verbatim.
    \end{itemize}
    Combining the two sub-cases, the lifted edge $(v_1^1, v_2^0)$ from $(v_1, v_2) \in E$ survives the RHS removal iff $v_2 \notin W_1$, matching the LHS surviving condition exactly. (In the $v_2 \in W_2$ branch this is supplied automatically by the disjointness $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$; in the $v_2 \in V \sm W_2$ branch it is read off the unsplit identification.)
    \item \emph{Transfer edges $(w^0, w^1)$ for $w \in W_2$.}
    The head is $w^1 \in W_2^1$, a tagged copy in the disjoint-union piece $W_2^1$. By def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} item ii.'s tagged-construction disjointness $W_2^1 \cap (J \cup V) = \emptyset$, and since $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$, we have $w^1 \notin W_1$ (in the split-graph carrier). Hence every transfer edge survives the RHS removal; the LHS contributes the same transfer edges $\lC (w^0, w^1) \st w \in W_2 \rC$ unchanged.
\end{itemize}
Therefore
\[
    E_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}}
    \;=\; \lC (v_1^1, v_2^0) \st (v_1, v_2) \in E,\; v_2 \notin W_1 \rC \;\cup\; \lC (w^0, w^1) \st w \in W_2 \rC.
\]
Comparing with the LHS,
\[
    E_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}}
    \;=\; \lC (v_1^1, v_2^0) \st (v_1, v_2) \in E,\; v_2 \notin W_1 \rC \;\cup\; \lC (w^0, w^1) \st w \in W_2 \rC
    \;=\; E_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}}.
\]
\checkmark

\medskip\noindent\emph{Bidirected edges.}

\smallskip\noindent\emph{Refactor-context note on the removal clause of $\doit$ for $L$.}
At the LN level the bidirected-edge set $L$ consists of unordered pairs of distinct vertices in $V$, and def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} item iv.\ removes those pairs that touch $W_1$. The LN's literal one-sided gloss ``$L \sm \lC v \huh w \mid w \in W_1 \rC$'' selects an ordered-pair representative of each unordered pair and tests its second component, which presumes an ordered-pair encoding of $L$. The post-refactor encoding of def \ref{def-cdmg} (and downstream def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention}) carries $L$ as a set of \emph{unordered} pairs, so there is no ordered ``second component'' to single out: the structurally well-defined reading of the LN's removal clause is the endpoint-universal form
\[
    L_{\doit(W)} \;=\; \lC v_1 \huh v_2 \in L \st v_1 \notin W,\; v_2 \notin W \rC,
\]
i.e.\ ``$v_1 \huh v_2$ survives iff every endpoint of the unordered pair lies outside $W$''. This is precisely the LN's intended ``remove all edges into nodes from $W$'' applied to bidirected edges (which are incident to \emph{both} endpoints) and agrees on the survivor set with the LN's literal one-sided gloss after closing under the implicit swap of representatives. The pre-refactor encoding of def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} had to spell this symmetrisation out as a registered deviation; under the post-refactor unordered-pair encoding the LN-faithful reading is the literal one and no symmetrisation step is required. We use the endpoint-universal form throughout the calculations below.

By def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} item iv.\ (endpoint-universal form), applied to $G$ with intervention set $W_1$,
\[
    L_{G_{\doit(W_1)}}
    \;=\; \lC v_1 \huh v_2 \in L \st v_1 \notin W_1,\; v_2 \notin W_1 \rC.
\]
By def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} item iv.\ applied to $G_{\doit(W_1)}$ with split set $W_2$ (each surviving bidirected edge $v_1 \huh v_2 \in L_{G_{\doit(W_1)}}$ is lifted to $v_1^0 \huh v_2^0$, with \emph{both} endpoints carrying the $^0$ superscript),
\[
    L_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}}
    \;=\; \lC v_1^0 \huh v_2^0 \st v_1 \huh v_2 \in L_{G_{\doit(W_1)}} \rC
    \;=\; \lC v_1^0 \huh v_2^0 \st v_1 \huh v_2 \in L,\; v_1 \notin W_1,\; v_2 \notin W_1 \rC.
\]

For the RHS, by def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} item iv.\ applied to $G$ with split set $W_2$,
\[
    L_{G_{\spl(W_2)}}
    \;=\; \lC v_1^0 \huh v_2^0 \st v_1 \huh v_2 \in L \rC.
\]
By def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} item iv.\ (endpoint-universal form), applied to $G_{\spl(W_2)}$ with intervention set $W_1$ (lifted to the split-graph carrier via the unsplit injection),
\[
    L_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}}
    \;=\; \lC a \huh b \in L_{G_{\spl(W_2)}} \st a \notin W_1,\; b \notin W_1 \rC.
\]
We compute the surviving pairs by analysing each endpoint of the generator $v_1^0 \huh v_2^0$ arising from $v_1 \huh v_2 \in L$. Since $L$ consists of unordered pairs of distinct vertices in $V$, both $v_1, v_2 \in V$. For each $k \in \{1, 2\}$:
\begin{itemize}
    \item \emph{If $v_k \in W_2$:} $v_k^0 \in W_2^0$ is a tagged copy. By def \ref{def:G_node-splitting} item ii.'s tagged-construction disjointness $W_2^0 \cap (J \cup V) = \emptyset$, and since $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$, we have $v_k^0 \notin W_1$ (in the split-graph carrier). Cross-check with the LHS: by the disjointness hypothesis $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$, $v_k \in W_2$ also forces $v_k \notin W_1$, so both the RHS-side condition ``$v_k^0 \notin W_1$'' and the LHS-side condition ``$v_k \notin W_1$'' are satisfied automatically in this branch.
    \item \emph{If $v_k \in V \sm W_2$:} $v_k^0 = v_k$ (unsplit shorthand, since $v_k \in V \sm W_2 \subseteq J \cup (V \sm W_2)$). The lifted $W_1$ in the split-graph carrier is the unsplit image $\lC w \st w \in W_1 \rC$, so $v_k^0 \in W_1$ iff $v_k \in W_1$ (the unsplit injection is injective). Hence the RHS-side condition ``$v_k^0 \notin W_1$'' is equivalent to the LHS-side condition ``$v_k \notin W_1$''.
\end{itemize}
In both sub-cases the condition ``$v_k^0 \notin W_1$'' is equivalent to ``$v_k \notin W_1$''. Applying this to both endpoints $v_1$ and $v_2$ independently,
\[
    L_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}}
    \;=\; \lC v_1^0 \huh v_2^0 \st v_1 \huh v_2 \in L,\; v_1 \notin W_1,\; v_2 \notin W_1 \rC.
\]
Comparing with the LHS,
\[
    L_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}}
    \;=\; \lC v_1^0 \huh v_2^0 \st v_1 \huh v_2 \in L,\; v_1 \notin W_1,\; v_2 \notin W_1 \rC
    \;=\; L_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}}.
\]
\checkmark

\smallskip
Combining the four componentwise equalities ($J$, $V$, $E$, $L$) above, the CDMGs $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}$ and $\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}$ agree field by field on each of $(J, V, E, L)$ and are therefore equal as CDMGs (def \ref{def-cdmg}).
\end{proof}

\end{document}
tex_proof.html:
Lemma — Disjoint hard interventions and node-splittings commute

Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def def-cdmg); throughout we use the notation of def not-cdmg and recall the typing and axioms of def def-cdmg, namely $J \cap V = \emptyset$, $E \subseteq (J \cup V) \times V$, and $L$ consists of unordered pairs of distinct vertices in $V$. Let \[ W_1 \subseteq J \cup V \quad\text{and}\quad W_2 \subseteq V \] be two subsets of nodes of $G$, subject to the disjointness side condition \[ W_1 \cap W_2 \;=\; \emptyset. \] All three quantifiers “for every CDMG $G$”, “for every $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$”, and “for every $W_2 \subseteq V$ with $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$” are read universally; in particular the (vacuously disjoint) case $W_1 = W_2 = \emptyset$ is admitted.

Well-typedness of the inner splitting on the LHS. The LHS $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}$ requires its outer $\spl(W_2)$ to act on a CDMG whose output-node set contains $W_2$, i.e.\ $W_2 \subseteq V_{\doit(W_1)}$ per def def:G_node-splitting's precondition. By item ii.\ of def def:G_hard_intervention we have $V_{\doit(W_1)} = V \sm W_1$, and the inclusion \[ W_2 \;\subseteq\; V \sm W_1 \;=\; V_{\doit(W_1)} \] follows from $W_2 \subseteq V$ together with $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$. Hence the LHS is defined per def def:G_node-splitting. Well-typedness of the RHS $\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}$ is spelled out in the carrier-reading paragraph below the displayed equality.

The conclusion. The CDMG obtained from first hard intervening on $W_1$ and then node-splitting $W_2$ equals the CDMG obtained from first node-splitting $W_2$ and then hard intervening on $W_1$: \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)} \;=\; \lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}. \] The equality is understood componentwise on the four components $(J, V, E, L)$ of def def-cdmg; the conjunctive unpacking into the four field-by-field equalities is deferred to the proof.

Carrier reading of the equality. By def def:G_node-splitting the operation $\spl(W_2)$ shifts the carrier from $J \cup V$ to the tagged-disjoint-union carrier \[ J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)} \;=\; J \;\cup\; \lp (V \sm W_2) \dcup W_2^0 \dcup W_2^1 \rp, \] while by def def:G_hard_intervention the operation $\doit(W)$ preserves the carrier $J \cup V$ of its input as a set (items i.\ and ii.\ give $J_{\doit(W)} \cup V_{\doit(W)} = (J \cup W) \cup (V \sm W) = J \cup V$ whenever $W \subseteq J \cup V$). Thus both sides of the displayed equality live in a CDMG over the same $\spl(W_2)$-carrier just displayed, and the asserted equality is a literal $=$ of CDMGs (def def-cdmg) — not an equality up to a carrier-relabelling map. On the right-hand side, $\doit(W_1)$ is read as hard-intervening on the unsplit copies of the nodes of $W_1$ inside the split-graph carrier: every $w \in W_1$ satisfies $w \in J \cup V$ (by $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$) and $w \notin W_2$ (by $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$), so $w \in J \cup (V \sm W_2)$, which is the untagged piece of $J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)}$ under def def:G_node-splitting's notational shorthand “$v^0 := v^1 := v$ for $v \in J \cup (V \sm W_2)$” (i.e.\ $W_1$ injects into the split-graph carrier as the unsplit injection $w \mapsto w$). The precondition $W_1 \subseteq J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)}$ of def def:G_hard_intervention is thereby met by the unsplit copies, so the RHS is also defined per def def:G_hard_intervention, and the LN's $G_{\spl(W_2)}$ carrier reading agrees with the unique LN-faithful reading of $\doit(W_1)$ on it.

Proof.

We verify the equality $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)} = \lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}$ componentwise on the four fields $(J, V, E, L)$ of def def-cdmg, organised as in the LN under three section headings: “Node sets” (covering $J$ and $V$ jointly), “Directed edges” (the $E$ field), and “Bidirected edges” (the $L$ field). The well-typedness of both iteration orders has been established in the statement block above: the LHS by $W_2 \subseteq V \sm W_1 = V_{\doit(W_1)}$ (using $W_2 \subseteq V$ and the disjointness $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$), and the RHS by $W_1 \subseteq J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)}$ via the unsplit injection of $W_1$ into the split-graph carrier (using $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$ and $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$).

Notational reminder. For $w \in W_2$ we write $w^0$, $w^1$ for the two tagged copies produced by $\spl(W_2)$ per def def:G_node-splitting item ii.; these live in the tagged-disjoint-union pieces $W_2^0$ and $W_2^1$ respectively, and are formally distinct from every element of $J \cup V$ (and from each other), per def def:G_node-splitting's tagged-copies disjointness assertions $W_2^0 \cap V = W_2^1 \cap V = W_2^0 \cap J = W_2^1 \cap J = W_2^0 \cap W_2^1 = \emptyset$. For $v \in J \cup (V \sm W_2)$ we use def def:G_node-splitting's unsplit shorthand $v^0 := v^1 := v$, which leaves $v$ in the untagged piece of $J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)}$. On the right-hand side, the outer $\doit(W_1)$ is read on the lifted intervention set “$W_1$ inside the split-graph carrier”, which by the carrier-reading paragraph of the statement is the unsplit image $\lC w \st w \in W_1 \rC \subseteq J \cup (V \sm W_2)$ (using $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$ and $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$); throughout the proof we write $W_1$ for this lifted set, using the unsplit shorthand $w = w^0 = w^1$ for $w \in W_1$. “Head” of a directed edge $(a, b) \in E$ means its second component $b$; “endpoints” of a bidirected edge $v_1 \huh v_2 \in L$ means the unordered pair $\{v_1, v_2\}$ of its two vertices.

Node sets.

Input nodes ($J$). By def def:G_hard_intervention item i.\ applied to $G$ with intervention set $W_1$, \[ J_{G_{\doit(W_1)}} \;=\; J \cup W_1. \] By def def:G_node-splitting item i.\ applied to $G_{\doit(W_1)}$ with split set $W_2$ (well-typed by $W_2 \subseteq V \sm W_1 = V_{\doit(W_1)}$), \[ J_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}} \;=\; J_{G_{\doit(W_1)}} \;=\; J \cup W_1. \] For the RHS, by def def:G_node-splitting item i.\ applied to $G$ with split set $W_2$ and then def def:G_hard_intervention item i.\ applied to $G_{\spl(W_2)}$ with intervention set $W_1$ (lifted via the unsplit injection), \[ J_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}} \;=\; J_{G_{\spl(W_2)}} \cup W_1 \;=\; J \cup W_1. \] Hence \[ J_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}} \;=\; J \cup W_1 \;=\; J_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}}. \]

Output nodes ($V$). By def def:G_hard_intervention item ii.\ applied to $G$ with intervention set $W_1$ and then def def:G_node-splitting item ii.\ applied to $G_{\doit(W_1)}$ with split set $W_2 \subseteq V \sm W_1 = V_{\doit(W_1)}$, \[ V_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}} \;=\; \lp V_{G_{\doit(W_1)}} \sm W_2 \rp \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1 \;=\; \lp (V \sm W_1) \sm W_2 \rp \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1, \] which simplifies (by $(V \sm W_1) \sm W_2 = V \sm W_1 \sm W_2$, the standard “two-step removal” identity for set-difference) to \[ V_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}} \;=\; (V \sm W_1 \sm W_2) \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1. \]

For the RHS, by def def:G_node-splitting item ii.\ applied to $G$ with split set $W_2$ and then def def:G_hard_intervention item ii.\ applied to $G_{\spl(W_2)}$ with intervention set $W_1$ (lifted to the split-graph carrier via the unsplit injection), \[ V_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}} \;=\; V_{G_{\spl(W_2)}} \sm W_1 \;=\; \lp (V \sm W_2) \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1 \rp \sm W_1. \] We simplify the latter by decomposing the set-difference across the three disjoint pieces of the disjoint union, using both the disjointness hypothesis $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$ (at the untagged piece) and def def:G_node-splitting's tagged-construction disjointness (at the tagged pieces):

  • $W_1 \cap W_2^0 = \emptyset$ and $W_1 \cap W_2^1 = \emptyset$: by def def:G_node-splitting item ii.'s tagged-construction disjointness, $W_2^0$ and $W_2^1$ satisfy $W_2^0 \cap (J \cup V) = W_2^1 \cap (J \cup V) = \emptyset$. Since $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$ (and the lifted $W_1$ in the split-graph carrier sits in the untagged piece $J \cup (V \sm W_2)$, which is a subset of $J \cup V$ under the unsplit injection), the lifted $W_1$ does not meet $W_2^0$ or $W_2^1$. Hence the set-difference acts as the identity on both tagged pieces.
  • $(V \sm W_2) \sm W_1 = V \sm W_1 \sm W_2$: indeed, for any $x$, the chain of equivalences $x \in (V \sm W_2) \sm W_1$ iff $x \in V$, $x \notin W_2$, $x \notin W_1$ iff $x \in V$, $x \notin W_1$, $x \notin W_2$ iff $x \in V \sm W_1 \sm W_2$ holds. (Here the disjointness $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$ is not needed for this equality; the two iterated removals commute by associativity of $\sm$ alone.)

Combining the three piecewise calculations, \[ \lp (V \sm W_2) \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1 \rp \sm W_1 \;=\; \lp (V \sm W_2) \sm W_1 \rp \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1 \;=\; (V \sm W_1 \sm W_2) \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1. \] Hence \[ V_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}} \;=\; (V \sm W_1 \sm W_2) \;\dcup\; W_2^0 \;\dcup\; W_2^1 \;=\; V_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}}. \] ✓

Directed edges. By def def:G_hard_intervention item iii.\ applied to $G$ with intervention set $W_1$, \[ E_{G_{\doit(W_1)}} \;=\; E \sm \lC (v_1, v_2) \in E \st v_2 \in W_1 \rC \;=\; \lC (v_1, v_2) \in E \st v_2 \notin W_1 \rC. \] By def def:G_node-splitting item iii.\ applied to $G_{\doit(W_1)}$ with split set $W_2$ (each surviving directed edge $(v_1, v_2) \in E_{G_{\doit(W_1)}}$ is lifted to $(v_1^1, v_2^0)$, and each $w \in W_2$ contributes a transfer edge $(w^0, w^1)$), \[\begin{aligned} E_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}} &= \lC (v_1^1, v_2^0) \st (v_1, v_2) \in E_{G_{\doit(W_1)}} \rC \;\cup\; \lC (w^0, w^1) \st w \in W_2 \rC \\ &= \lC (v_1^1, v_2^0) \st (v_1, v_2) \in E,\; v_2 \notin W_1 \rC \;\cup\; \lC (w^0, w^1) \st w \in W_2 \rC. \end{aligned}\]

For the RHS, by def def:G_node-splitting item iii.\ applied to $G$ with split set $W_2$, \[ E_{G_{\spl(W_2)}} \;=\; \lC (v_1^1, v_2^0) \st (v_1, v_2) \in E \rC \;\cup\; \lC (w^0, w^1) \st w \in W_2 \rC. \] By def def:G_hard_intervention item iii.\ applied to $G_{\spl(W_2)}$ with intervention set $W_1$ (lifted to the split-graph carrier via the unsplit injection), \[ E_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}} \;=\; E_{G_{\spl(W_2)}} \sm \lC (a, b) \in E_{G_{\spl(W_2)}} \st b \in W_1 \rC. \] The removal condition “$b \in W_1$” inspects the head $b$ of each candidate generator. We analyse it case by case on the two generator types of $E_{G_{\spl(W_2)}}$.

  • Lifted edges $(v_1^1, v_2^0)$ from $(v_1, v_2) \in E$. The head is $v_2^0$. By def def-cdmg's typing $E \subseteq (J \cup V) \times V$ we have $v_2 \in V$, so we case-split on whether $v_2 \in W_2$: \begin{itemize}
  • If $v_2 \in W_2$: $v_2^0 \in W_2^0$ is a tagged copy, lying in the disjoint-union piece $W_2^0$. By def def:G_node-splitting item ii.'s tagged-construction disjointness $W_2^0 \cap (J \cup V) = \emptyset$, and since $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$, we have $v_2^0 \notin W_1$ (read in the split-graph carrier via the unsplit lift). The edge survives the RHS removal. Cross-check with the LHS: by the disjointness hypothesis $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$, $v_2 \in W_2$ also forces $v_2 \notin W_1$, so the LHS-side condition “$v_2 \notin W_1$” is automatically met — both sides keep this edge.
  • If $v_2 \in V \sm W_2$: $v_2^0 = v_2$ (unsplit shorthand, since $v_2 \in V \sm W_2 \subseteq J \cup (V \sm W_2)$). The lifted $W_1$ in the split-graph carrier is the unsplit image $\lC w \st w \in W_1 \rC$, so $v_2^0 \in W_1$ iff $v_2 \in W_1$ (the unsplit injection $J \cup (V \sm W_2) \hookrightarrow J_{\spl(W_2)} \cup V_{\spl(W_2)}$ is injective). The edge is removed by the RHS iff $v_2 \in W_1$, equivalently kept iff $v_2 \notin W_1$. This matches the LHS condition “$v_2 \notin W_1$” verbatim. \end{itemize} Combining the two sub-cases, the lifted edge $(v_1^1, v_2^0)$ from $(v_1, v_2) \in E$ survives the RHS removal iff $v_2 \notin W_1$, matching the LHS surviving condition exactly. (In the $v_2 \in W_2$ branch this is supplied automatically by the disjointness $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$; in the $v_2 \in V \sm W_2$ branch it is read off the unsplit identification.)
  • Transfer edges $(w^0, w^1)$ for $w \in W_2$. The head is $w^1 \in W_2^1$, a tagged copy in the disjoint-union piece $W_2^1$. By def def:G_node-splitting item ii.'s tagged-construction disjointness $W_2^1 \cap (J \cup V) = \emptyset$, and since $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$, we have $w^1 \notin W_1$ (in the split-graph carrier). Hence every transfer edge survives the RHS removal; the LHS contributes the same transfer edges $\lC (w^0, w^1) \st w \in W_2 \rC$ unchanged.

Therefore \[ E_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}} \;=\; \lC (v_1^1, v_2^0) \st (v_1, v_2) \in E,\; v_2 \notin W_1 \rC \;\cup\; \lC (w^0, w^1) \st w \in W_2 \rC. \] Comparing with the LHS, \[ E_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}} \;=\; \lC (v_1^1, v_2^0) \st (v_1, v_2) \in E,\; v_2 \notin W_1 \rC \;\cup\; \lC (w^0, w^1) \st w \in W_2 \rC \;=\; E_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}}. \] ✓

Bidirected edges.

Refactor-context note on the removal clause of $\doit$ for $L$. At the LN level the bidirected-edge set $L$ consists of unordered pairs of distinct vertices in $V$, and def def:G_hard_intervention item iv.\ removes those pairs that touch $W_1$. The LN's literal one-sided gloss “$L \sm \lC v \huh w \mid w \in W_1 \rC$” selects an ordered-pair representative of each unordered pair and tests its second component, which presumes an ordered-pair encoding of $L$. The post-refactor encoding of def def-cdmg (and downstream def def:G_hard_intervention) carries $L$ as a set of unordered pairs, so there is no ordered “second component” to single out: the structurally well-defined reading of the LN's removal clause is the endpoint-universal form \[ L_{\doit(W)} \;=\; \lC v_1 \huh v_2 \in L \st v_1 \notin W,\; v_2 \notin W \rC, \] i.e.\ “$v_1 \huh v_2$ survives iff every endpoint of the unordered pair lies outside $W$”. This is precisely the LN's intended “remove all edges into nodes from $W$” applied to bidirected edges (which are incident to both endpoints) and agrees on the survivor set with the LN's literal one-sided gloss after closing under the implicit swap of representatives. The pre-refactor encoding of def def:G_hard_intervention had to spell this symmetrisation out as a registered deviation; under the post-refactor unordered-pair encoding the LN-faithful reading is the literal one and no symmetrisation step is required. We use the endpoint-universal form throughout the calculations below.

By def def:G_hard_intervention item iv.\ (endpoint-universal form), applied to $G$ with intervention set $W_1$, \[ L_{G_{\doit(W_1)}} \;=\; \lC v_1 \huh v_2 \in L \st v_1 \notin W_1,\; v_2 \notin W_1 \rC. \] By def def:G_node-splitting item iv.\ applied to $G_{\doit(W_1)}$ with split set $W_2$ (each surviving bidirected edge $v_1 \huh v_2 \in L_{G_{\doit(W_1)}}$ is lifted to $v_1^0 \huh v_2^0$, with both endpoints carrying the $^0$ superscript), \[ L_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}} \;=\; \lC v_1^0 \huh v_2^0 \st v_1 \huh v_2 \in L_{G_{\doit(W_1)}} \rC \;=\; \lC v_1^0 \huh v_2^0 \st v_1 \huh v_2 \in L,\; v_1 \notin W_1,\; v_2 \notin W_1 \rC. \]

For the RHS, by def def:G_node-splitting item iv.\ applied to $G$ with split set $W_2$, \[ L_{G_{\spl(W_2)}} \;=\; \lC v_1^0 \huh v_2^0 \st v_1 \huh v_2 \in L \rC. \] By def def:G_hard_intervention item iv.\ (endpoint-universal form), applied to $G_{\spl(W_2)}$ with intervention set $W_1$ (lifted to the split-graph carrier via the unsplit injection), \[ L_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}} \;=\; \lC a \huh b \in L_{G_{\spl(W_2)}} \st a \notin W_1,\; b \notin W_1 \rC. \] We compute the surviving pairs by analysing each endpoint of the generator $v_1^0 \huh v_2^0$ arising from $v_1 \huh v_2 \in L$. Since $L$ consists of unordered pairs of distinct vertices in $V$, both $v_1, v_2 \in V$. For each $k \in \{1, 2\}$:

  • If $v_k \in W_2$: $v_k^0 \in W_2^0$ is a tagged copy. By def def:G_node-splitting item ii.'s tagged-construction disjointness $W_2^0 \cap (J \cup V) = \emptyset$, and since $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$, we have $v_k^0 \notin W_1$ (in the split-graph carrier). Cross-check with the LHS: by the disjointness hypothesis $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$, $v_k \in W_2$ also forces $v_k \notin W_1$, so both the RHS-side condition “$v_k^0 \notin W_1$” and the LHS-side condition “$v_k \notin W_1$” are satisfied automatically in this branch.
  • If $v_k \in V \sm W_2$: $v_k^0 = v_k$ (unsplit shorthand, since $v_k \in V \sm W_2 \subseteq J \cup (V \sm W_2)$). The lifted $W_1$ in the split-graph carrier is the unsplit image $\lC w \st w \in W_1 \rC$, so $v_k^0 \in W_1$ iff $v_k \in W_1$ (the unsplit injection is injective). Hence the RHS-side condition “$v_k^0 \notin W_1$” is equivalent to the LHS-side condition “$v_k \notin W_1$”.

In both sub-cases the condition “$v_k^0 \notin W_1$” is equivalent to “$v_k \notin W_1$”. Applying this to both endpoints $v_1$ and $v_2$ independently, \[ L_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}} \;=\; \lC v_1^0 \huh v_2^0 \st v_1 \huh v_2 \in L,\; v_1 \notin W_1,\; v_2 \notin W_1 \rC. \] Comparing with the LHS, \[ L_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}} \;=\; \lC v_1^0 \huh v_2^0 \st v_1 \huh v_2 \in L,\; v_1 \notin W_1,\; v_2 \notin W_1 \rC \;=\; L_{\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}}. \] ✓

Combining the four componentwise equalities ($J$, $V$, $E$, $L$) above, the CDMGs $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\spl(W_2)}$ and $\lp G_{\spl(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}$ agree field by field on each of $(J, V, E, L)$ and are therefore equal as CDMGs (def def-cdmg).

lean_blocks.explanation: This is a `variable` declaration: Lean auto-binds `Node : Type*` and the instance `[DecidableEq Node]` as implicit arguments to every subsequent declaration in the surrounding `CDMG` namespace, sparing us from rewriting the same parameters on the two helpers and the main theorem. The `Type*` notation makes `Node` universe-polymorphic, so callers can instantiate it at any universe level. The `DecidableEq Node` assumption is load-bearing for this row: the proof manipulates `Finset.filter`, `Finset.union`, `Finset.sdiff`, and `Finset.image` over both `Node` and `SplitNode Node`, all of which need decidable equality to compute the resulting `Finset`. The split-graph carrier `SplitNode Node` inherits `[DecidableEq (SplitNode Node)]` automatically via the `deriving DecidableEq` clause on `SplitNode`, so the single ambient assumption propagates through both iteration orders of $\doit$ and $\spl$. Stronger structure on `Node` (e.g.\ `Fintype`, `LinearOrder`) is not required here. Same convention as the rest of `Chapter3_GraphTheory`'s files.

-- Auto-binds `Node` + `DecidableEq Node` as implicits; decidability drives `Finset.filter`/`union`/`sdiff`/`image` on both `Node` and `SplitNode Node`.
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]

lean_blocks.code:
private lemma subset_V_of_hardInterventionOn
    {G : CDMG Node} {W₁ : Finset Node} (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V)
    {W₂ : Finset Node} (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V) (hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    W₂ ⊆ (G.hardInterventionOn W₁ hW₁).V
lean_blocks.explanation: This block is the first of two load-bearing statement helpers for the mixed commutativity claim (one `hardInterventionOn` + one `nodeSplittingOn`, on disjoint node sets). The `private lemma` records that for $W_1, W_2$ with $W_2 \subseteq V$ disjoint from $W_1$, the set $W_2$ still lies inside the output-node set of $G_{\doit(W_1)}$ — concretely $W_2 \subseteq (G.do(W_1)).V$. The fact is needed inside the main theorem's signature to type the call `(G.hardInterventionOn W₁ ...).nodeSplittingOn W₂ ...`, which demands $W_2 \subseteq V$ on the intervened carrier. Disjointness of $W_1$ and $W_2$ is what guarantees $W_2$ doesn't get sucked into $W_1$ during the intervention's set-difference $V \setminus W_1$. The marker pair wraps the signature only (signature-only helper-wrap convention); the `:= by ...` tactic proof body sits below the end marker and routes elementhood through `Finset.mem_sdiff` to land each $w \in W_2$ in $G.V$ minus $W_1$.

-- ref: claim_3_8 (helper) — statement-supporting subset lemma for the mixed commutativity claim.
-- For `W₁, W₂` with `W₂ ⊆ V` disjoint from `W₁`, `W₂` survives `hardInterventionOn W₁` on the V-side.
private lemma subset_V_of_hardInterventionOn
    {G : CDMG Node} {W₁ : Finset Node} (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V)
    {W₂ : Finset Node} (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V) (hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    W₂ ⊆ (G.hardInterventionOn W₁ hW₁).V

lean_blocks.code:
private lemma image_unsplit_subset_carrier_of_nodeSplittingOn
    {G : CDMG Node} {W₁ : Finset Node} (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V)
    {W₂ : Finset Node} (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V) (hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    W₁.image SplitNode.unsplit ⊆
      (G.nodeSplittingOn W₂ hW₂).J ∪
        (G.nodeSplittingOn W₂ hW₂).V
lean_blocks.explanation: This is the second of two statement-supporting `private lemma` helpers for the mixed commutativity claim. It records that for $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$ and $W_2 \subseteq V$ with `Disjoint W₁ W₂`, the unsplit-lifted set `W₁.image SplitNode.unsplit` sits inside the carrier `(G.nodeSplittingOn W₂ hW₂).J ∪ (G.nodeSplittingOn W₂ hW₂).V` of the inner node-splitting. The fact is needed inside the main theorem's signature to type the RHS call `(G.nodeSplittingOn W₂ hW₂).hardInterventionOn (W₁.image SplitNode.unsplit) ?_`. The lift via `Finset.image SplitNode.unsplit` realises def 3.11's shorthand $v^0 := v^1 := v$ for $v \in J \cup (V \setminus W_2)$ at the Lean type level — each $w \in W_1$ is tagged as an untagged-piece node of the split-graph carrier. Disjointness is load-bearing only on the `V`-piece (to give $v \notin W_2$ when $v \in W_1 \cap G.V$); on the `J`-piece the lift lands directly in `G.J.image .unsplit` without using it. The marker pair wraps the signature only (signature-only helper-wrap convention); the `:= by ...` tactic body sits below and routes elementhood through a `rcases` on `Finset.mem_union.mp (hW₁ hv)` followed by `Finset.disjoint_left.mp hDisj` in the V-branch.

-- ref: claim_3_8 (helper) — statement-supporting carrier-subset lemma for the RHS's outer `hardInterventionOn`.
-- For `W₁ ⊆ J ∪ V`, `W₂ ⊆ V` disjoint from `W₁`, `W₁.image .unsplit` lands inside the split-graph carrier.
private lemma image_unsplit_subset_carrier_of_nodeSplittingOn
    -- Implicit `G` and `W₁`; explicit `hW₁` records the LN's `W₁ ⊆ J ∪ V` precondition.
    {G : CDMG Node} {W₁ : Finset Node} (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V)
    -- Implicit `W₂`; explicit `hW₂` records `W₂ ⊆ V` (the `nodeSplittingOn` precondition).
    {W₂ : Finset Node} (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V) (hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    -- Goal: the unsplit-image of `W₁` is contained in `(split).J ∪ (split).V`.
    W₁.image SplitNode.unsplit ⊆
      (G.nodeSplittingOn W₂ hW₂).J ∪
        (G.nodeSplittingOn W₂ hW₂).V

lean_blocks.code:
theorem disjointHardInterventionsAndNodeSplittingsCommute
    (G : CDMG Node) (W₁ W₂ : Finset Node)
    (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V) (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V) (hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    (G.hardInterventionOn W₁ hW₁).nodeSplittingOn W₂
        (subset_V_of_hardInterventionOn hW₁ hW₂ hDisj)
      = (G.nodeSplittingOn W₂ hW₂).hardInterventionOn
          (W₁.image SplitNode.unsplit)
          (image_unsplit_subset_carrier_of_nodeSplittingOn hW₁ hW₂ hDisj)
lean_blocks.explanation: This is the main declaration of `claim_3_8`: the LN lemma that disjoint hard interventions and node-splittings commute, formalised as a literal `=` of `CDMG (SplitNode Node)`. The signature exposes the universal quantification over a graph `G : CDMG Node` and two `Finset Node` subsets `W₁`, `W₂`, plus the three hypotheses `hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V`, `hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V`, `hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂`. The two outer constructor calls discharge their inner-`hW` proof obligations by passing the two private helpers `subset_V_of_hardInterventionOn` and `image_unsplit_subset_carrier_of_nodeSplittingOn` declared above, both consuming `hDisj`. The body — wrapped between the `-- claim_3_8 -- start statement` / `-- end statement` markers at signature level only — proves the equality via a local `cdmgExt` extensionality (eight fields under the post-`cdmg_typed_edges` shape) and a `toCopy0_notMem_iff` iff that bridges "unsplit copy avoids `W₁.image .unsplit`" with "original avoids `W₁`". The L-sub-goal exploits `Sym2.map`'s definitional swap-symmetry, threading the endpoint-universal filter `∀ v ∈ s, v ∉ W₁` through `Finset.filter_image` and `Finset.filter_congr`. No `eqViaNodeMap` workaround is needed.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Main theorem — disjoint hard interventions and node-splittings commute as a literal `=` of CDMGs.
theorem disjointHardInterventionsAndNodeSplittingsCommute
    -- Quantifies over a CDMG `G` over `Node` and two `Finset` node subsets `W₁`, `W₂`.
    (G : CDMG Node) (W₁ W₂ : Finset Node)
    -- LN preconditions: `W₁ ⊆ J ∪ V`, `W₂ ⊆ V`, and disjointness (Mathlib `Disjoint` form).
    (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V) (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V) (hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    -- LHS: first `hardInterventionOn W₁` on `G`, then `nodeSplittingOn W₂` on the result.
    (G.hardInterventionOn W₁ hW₁).nodeSplittingOn W₂
        -- Inner-`hW` for the outer splitting: `W₂` survives the intervention; discharged by the first helper.
        (subset_V_of_hardInterventionOn hW₁ hW₂ hDisj)
      -- RHS: first `nodeSplittingOn W₂` on `G`, then `hardInterventionOn` on the unsplit-lifted `W₁`.
      = (G.nodeSplittingOn W₂ hW₂).hardInterventionOn
          -- Lift `W₁` to the split-graph carrier via `.image SplitNode.unsplit` (untagged-piece embedding).
          (W₁.image SplitNode.unsplit)
          -- Inner-`hW` for the outer intervention: lifted `W₁` lies in the split carrier; discharged by the second helper.
          (image_unsplit_subset_carrier_of_nodeSplittingOn hW₁ hW₂ hDisj)

design_choices:
**Literal `=` of CDMGs over `SplitNode Node`, not `eqViaNodeMap` / `flattenSplit`.** Both sides take a *single* node-splitting on the same `W₂`, and `hardInterventionOn` preserves the node carrier (`CDMG α → CDMG α` per `def_3_10`), so both sides land in `CDMG (SplitNode Node)`: no carrier mismatch, and the asserted equality is a literal `=` between two terms of identical Lean type. Contrast `claim_3_7`, where iterating `nodeSplittingOn` twice produces `CDMG (SplitNode (SplitNode Node))` with constructor wrappings disagreeing between iteration orders, forcing the `eqViaNodeMap` / `flattenSplit` workaround. Mirrors `claim_3_4`'s literal-`=` pattern.

**Disjointness `Disjoint W₁ W₂` is load-bearing, not a convenience.** Two distinct uses in the signature: the LHS's inner `spl(W₂)` needs `W₂ ⊆ G.V ∖ W₁` (discharged by `subset_V_of_hardInterventionOn`); the RHS's outer `doit(W₁.image .unsplit)` needs the unsplit-lifted `W₁` inside the split-graph carrier (discharged by `image_unsplit_subset_carrier_of_nodeSplittingOn`). Without it a node $w \in W_1 \cap W_2$ would simultaneously be hard-intervened and split, committing the two orders to incompatible carrier placements. Encoded as Mathlib's `Disjoint`, feeding `Finset.disjoint_left.mp` / `_right.mp` in one step.

**Lift `W₁` via `Finset.image SplitNode.unsplit`.** Every $w \in W_1$ lies in $J \cup (V \setminus W_2)$ by `hW₁` and disjointness — the untagged piece of the split-graph carrier under `def_3_11`'s shorthand $v^0 := v^1 := v$. At the Lean level this piece is exactly the image of `SplitNode.unsplit`. Building a fresh `Finset (SplitNode Node)` and re-proving its carrier-subset relation was rejected because it discards the structural fact that no node of `W₁` is split. Same lift convention as `claim_3_7`.

**Standalone `private` helpers with marker pairs, not inline `by`-blocks.** The two outer constructor calls need *proof terms* for their inner-`hW` argument; inlining tactic blobs in the type would clutter the website-rendered signature and duplicate the same carrier-subset arithmetic at every future use site. `private` keeps the `CDMG` namespace clean; the markers pull each lemma onto the website alongside the statement.

**Single `theorem`, not a conjunction.** The LN statement is a single equality `LHS = RHS`. Unlike `claim_3_4` and `claim_3_7`, no joint-intervention form $G_{\mathrm{do}(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ exists: `doit` and `spl` are structurally distinct operations (different carriers, different field equations) and admit no combined invocation.

**Eight-field `cdmgExt` plus a local `Sym2`-`DecidablePred` instance, under `cdmg_typed_edges`.** `CDMG` has eight fields (no `hL_symm` — swap-symmetry is definitional on `Sym2`), and the L-filter `fun s : Sym2 _ => ∀ v ∈ s, v ∉ W` needs a `DecidablePred` that the `private` def-site copy does not propagate by `import`. A local polymorphic `disjointHardInterventions_decidable_bAll` covers both `Sym2 Node` and `Sym2 (SplitNode Node)` via `Sym2.recOnSubsingleton` + `Sym2.ball`. The L-sub-goal uses the endpoint-universal filter throughout; the `hard_intervention_l_symmetrized_removal` deviation that an ordered-pair `L`-encoding would force is structurally absent — `Sym2`'s definitional swap-symmetry resolves it at the `def_3_10` site.
